content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660           322-5901          322-6441              322-6444

December 22, 1982


Jon Q. Reynolds
2565 Merced Street
San Leandro, CA 94577


Dear Mr. Reynolds:

     I apologize for the delay in responding to your request for
information regarding your filing requirement as an elected
officeholder who has made contributions to other candidates.

     Government Code Section 84200 states in part:

     All elected officers shall file campaign statements
     no later than July 31 and January 31, provided that
     this requirement does not apply to elected officers
     whose salaries are less than one hundred dollars ($100)
     a month or judges, unless such elected officer is a
     candidate or committee which receives contributions
     or makes expenditures during the applicable six-month
     period. (Emphasis added)

     As an elected officeholder who has made expenditures (i.e.
contributions to other candidates) you must file a campaign statement
disclosing all receipts and expenditures since the closing date of your
last campaign statement, or January 1 if no previous statement has been
filed during the calendar year. Enclosed is a Form 430, Candidate and
Officeholder Campaign Statement.

     As stated in your letter, you also cosigned a note for a
candidate. You do not have any requirement to disclose that fact
unless you are ultimately called upon to repay the loan. (See enclosed
copy of a Commission adviser letter to Mr. Kirk Pesenti.) However, the
contributions from you and your wife to the other candidates must be
disclosed on the Form 430 as follows. First report the contributions
made from your personal funds as a miscellaneous increase to cash on
Schedule G, then as a payment on Schedule E. In addition, transfer the
information on Schedule E to the "Allocation Schedule" at the front of
the Form. (See page 14 of the enclosed Guide for Candidates and
Treasurers.)

Jon Q. Reynolds
Page two


     Since you are required to file campaign statements as an
elected officeholder, you have no other filing obligations.  Also,
assuming that the contributions were made from community funds, your
wife would not have a filing obligation since the activity will be
disclosed on your Form 430.  However, once you have terminated your
status as an officeholder and you are no longer required to file
statements pursuant to Government Code Section 84200, you and your wife
will incur a filing obligation as a "Major Donor" committee once you
make contributions totaling $5,000 in a calendar year.  (Gov. Code
Section 82013(c).)

     If I can be of any further assistance to you, please do not
hesitate to contact me.  I assure you of a more timely response.

                                        Yours Very Truly,

                                        [signature]

                                        Lynn Montgomery
                                        Political Reform Consultant

LM:st
enclosures

JON Q. REYNOLDS

December 14, 1982


Ms. Lynn Montgomery
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICE BOARD
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, CA 95814


Dear Ms. Montgomery:

I have called you twice and you have promised to mail
me some forms for reporting but to date I have not
received them. I am becoming concerned about my
reporting. It is my understanding that I should
report on or before January 31 for the period ending
December 31. These are the circumstances:

I am an elected public official (East Bay Municipal
Utility District Ward #3) who chose not to run for
re-election. As an individual voter, my wife and I
donated to various campaigns in an aggregate amount
of amount $6,000, about $3,000 each. In addition,
I co-signed a note for a candidate for East Bay
Municipal Utility District for the office that I
vacated. The amount of the note was $40,000 to a
local bank. The candidate co-signed the note, also,
and the money was given to the candidate by the bank.
She is attempting to retire the note by other cam-
paign contributions. I don't know how successful
she has been. She has not called upon me to make
payment on the note.

How do I report this? Would you please send me the
proper form so that I would be in compliance? My
records show I called you on November 1 and you
returned my call on November 3 at 2:30 p.m. Your
office also returned my call on November 8. During
both instances, your office had agreed to mail me
the proper forms but I have not received them. Is
there a question as to which form I should use?
Should I go somewhere else to get the forms? I would
appreciate any help you could give me. Thank you
very much.

Yours truly,

Jon Q. Reynolds

REYNOLDS & BROWN

2595 MERCED STREET
SAN LEANDRO, CALIFORNIA 94577
AREA 415 351-4930

JON Q REYNOLDS
DAVID A BROWN
MILTON D REDFORD, JR

REPRESENTING
DELTA PROPERTIES
RBT INVESTMENT COMPANY
SAN LEANDRO INDUSTRIAL PARKS

(415) 827-3343

November 2, 1982


Ms. Jeannie Pritchard
Ms. Lynn Montgomery
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: Status as a Major Donor

My name is Jon Q. Reynolds, I am a retiring Director of the East Bay
Municipal Utility District, Ward 3, who is not seeking re-election.
During the recent campaign, I have made various contributions
totalling about $3,000, more or less. Recently, I co-signed a
bank loan for a candidate for my seat. Her name is Mary Warren.
The loan was made to her; I did not handle the funds, other than
to sign over the proceeds to her.

In the past, my annual donations have always been under $5,000 per
year.

I learned today of the requirement for major donors, being those who
contribute more than $5,000 per year to campaigns, to register with
you. Can you please explain to me the process by which I should
register as a major donor, should I now so qualify. Do you have
any forms for me to fill out. Your early reply to this request would
be appreciated.

November 2, 1982
Page 2


I called earlier today and you have my phone call on record. I asked
that either of you return my call should you get a moment. I know
that today is very hectic so that I can understand why you did not
get back to me.

I will await your reply.

Yours truly,

[signed] Don Q. Reynolds
Don Q. Reynolds

JQR:sg

sections.question_synthetic: What are the filing requirements for an elected officeholder who has made contributions to other candidates and cosigned a loan for a candidate?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: As an elected officeholder who made expenditures (contributions to other candidates), the recipient must file campaign statements disclosing all receipts and expenditures on Form 430. Cosigned loans need not be disclosed unless repayment is required. Contributions must be reported on Schedule G and E with allocation information, and the recipient's spouse has no separate filing obligation if contributions came from community funds.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard advice letter format without explicit 'QUESTION' and 'CONCLUSION' section headers; question and conclusion inferred from letter body. Document dated December 22, 1982. Includes practical filing guidance with form references.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the filing requirements for an elected officeholder who has made contributions to other candidates and cosigned a loan for a candidate?

CONCLUSION: As an elected officeholder who made expenditures (contributions to other candidates), the recipient must file campaign statements disclosing all receipts and expenditures on Form 430. Cosigned loans need not be disclosed unless repayment is required. Contributions must be reported on Schedule G and E with allocation information, and the recipient's spouse has no separate filing obligation if contributions came from community funds.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises elected officeholder Jon Q. Reynolds on his campaign filing requirements under Government Code Section 84200, clarifying that his contributions to other candidates trigger a filing obligation and explaining proper disclosure procedures on Form 430.
